title: 9 FX Drafts Settlement
body_text:
9	FX Drafts Settlement
9.1	Settlement between Central 1 and Your Financial Institution
Convera provides a settlement file to Central 1 at the end of each business day. Central 1 uses this file to debit your financial institution芒聙聶s Canadian dollar or US dollar Central 1 account. During enrollment, your financial institution decides whether all drafts will be settled in Canadian dollars or whether the currency of settlement (either Canadian or US dollars) will be selected at the time the draft is created.
The settlement amount is the wholesale value of all drafts your financial institution issued during the business day (including per item fees). The debit is either posted to a designated branch such as the head office or the branch that issued the draft(s), depending on the option selected by your financial institution.
If your financial institution opts to settle in US Dollars, there will be core platform fees. These fees are settled monthly via AFT to an account of your financial institution芒聙聶s choice. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725.
Your financial institution retains its revenue daily. There are two components of revenue: spread revenue and fee revenue.
FX Drafts settlement is displayed as code TD. It appears on BC and Ontario reports as follows:
BC financial institutions
芒聙聯 On the AIBC report (Account Activity and Balances 芒聙聯 Canadian) or the AIBU report (Account Activity and Balances 芒聙聯 US).
Ontario financial institutions
芒聙聯 On the AIOC report (Account Activity and Balances 芒聙聯 Canadian) or the AIOU report (Account Activity and Balances 芒聙聯 US).
Download these reports from Central 1芒聙聶s File and Report Exchange.
The AIBC and AIBU (BC financial institutions) are in the root directory.
The AIOC and AIOU (Ontario financial institutions) are available in the ONDL file that is in the root directory.
If applicable, PDFs of these reports are available in the Banking folder. See the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for information on accessing the File and Report Exchange.
Postings can also be viewed on the Central 1 Banking System. See the Current Account Services (CAS) Online Part for more information.
Related Information
Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule,
Form 2725
(available to Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario)
9.2	Settlement between Your Financial Institution and Your Member
Ensure that your member芒聙聶s account is debited before the draft is issued. Other AML-related requirements may apply if the draft is paid for in foreign funds and/or cash. Contact your AML Compliance Officer or FINTRAC for information. Financial institutions that subscribe to Central 1芒聙聶s Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part.
END
